registry_refs.notes: W&L rank 93; two registry rows resolve to the same WordPress publication.

publication_scope.notes: Other Harvard journal subdirectories on the shared host are excluded.

evidence.notes: Only /wp-admin/ is disallowed for user-agent *; Crawl-delay is 10 seconds and was honored.
One unpaginated WordPress page exposes 153 labeled issue links spanning Volume 1 (1964) through Volume 63 (2026). Current eras use issue pages; older entries link directly to whole-issue PDFs.
Current issue uses wp-block-columns records with linked title, author text, and direct wp-content PDF control; five primary works were visible.
Article page exposes h1.entry-title, author text at the start of .entry-content, an Abstract heading followed by abstract text, full HTML article text, and a direct PDF link.
Single smoke GET returned a 367,538-byte, eight-page PDF beginning with %PDF-1.3; SHA-256 582cfb41f815faecedd9fbe376c284067710e6587664dcdaf2e9818d1e7870bb.

reconnaissance.notes: Current discovery and PDF delivery pass, but historical layout transitions and whole-issue granularity require oldest/middle sampling before collection readiness.

include: Print-journal articles, policy essays, and student notes under /jol/
Issue and article PDFs linked by the journal archive
exclude: JOL Online and Markup Blog posts unless separately scoped
scope.notes: Archive labels, rather than WordPress upload dates, define issue attribution.

nodes.notes: Direct wp-content article or historical issue PDF.

edges.notes: Current issue-page URLs vary between root and /archive/ prefixes.
Older issue labels may resolve directly to whole-issue PDFs.
First column contains linked title and trailing author text.

archive_patterns.notes: Post date can corroborate year but issue heading remains authoritative.
Upload date is not necessarily publication date for retrospective uploads.

pagination.termination: Single page ends at Volume 1, Number 1 (January 1964).

layout_eras.label: WordPress issue and article pages
layout_eras.notes: Approximate transition: Volume 45 Number 2 is an issue page while Volume 45 Number 1 is a direct PDF; boundary needs sampling.

layout_eras.label: Archive labels linking directly to issue PDFs
layout_eras.notes: Granularity and embedded article metadata remain unsampled.

sources.example: The Congressional Review Act: Congress’s New Favorite Tool For Restoring Its Constitutional Authority

title.missingness: Present for all current sampled records; historical PDFs unsampled.

sources.locator: div.wp-block-columns div.wp-block-column:first-child p text after title link
sources.notes: Article page repeats author at start of .entry-content.

authors.missingness: Observed in newest issue; historical whole-issue PDFs unsampled.

sources.locator: .entry-content text after Abstract heading and before next section heading
sources.example: Recent Supreme Court decisions have reined in the runaway bureaucracy...
sources.notes: Abstract is not wrapped in a dedicated semantic container.

abstract.missingness: Present on sampled article page; historical availability unknown.

sources.notes: Prefer issue label over upload path.

year.missingness: Archive labels include years throughout observed range.

sources.notes: WordPress post date may differ from issue publication date.

date.missingness: Available for current post URLs only.

volume.missingness: Present on all archive issue labels.

issue.missingness: Present on all observed archive labels.

citation.missingness: No recommended-citation element observed in sampled HTML.

patterns.notes: Historical archive links appear to be issue-level; no historical file was downloaded.

validation.notes: Current sample is an eight-page article PDF matching the HTML title.

access.politeness_notes: Robots first, Crawl-delay 10 honored, then one archive, one issue, one article, and one PDF smoke request.

verdict.summary: Current WordPress issue/article discovery and direct article PDF delivery pass, while the historical archive changes to whole-issue PDFs and still requires representative sampling.
blockers: Oldest and middle whole-issue PDFs have not been sampled
Historical PDFs require article-level splitting or indexing policy
verdict.next_action: Sample one Volume 45 transition issue and one Volume 1 PDF, then define historical whole-issue splitting before collection.
verdict.implementation_notes: Use the archive as the sole seed, preserve its displayed volume/number/year, distinguish issue-page and direct-PDF links, and enforce the /jol/ path plus ten-second host delay.
